mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 55% in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that outpaces supply constraints, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and RSAFS both show positive sequential growth
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60d returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output and supply-side bottlenecks, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5d returns during recovery phases, with directional accuracy dropping below 48% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid CPI acceleration above 3.0% year-over-year forces equity investors to demand higher risk premiums, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 3.0% fail to predict negative 20d returns, with mean forward returns remaining positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Steady monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases household income and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 60d returns, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and retail sales confirm synchronized consumer and business investment demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both positive MoM
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to predict positive 5d returns with accuracy above 52%, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial production and rising GDP indicates supply chain constraints and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 20d returns during supply-constrained periods, with directional accuracy below 45% in out-of-sample tests.
